notes.title: Welcome to Beta
notes.body:
This is the Journery beta environment. Changes are deployed here first before going to prod.

Use this space to test new features — it resets to this seed content on every beta deploy.

notes.title: March 10
notes.body:
Good morning. Slept well. Big day ahead.

Things to get done:
  • Call the bank about the statement
  • Review the design mockups before standup
  • Pick up groceries on the way home

Feeling optimistic about the week overall.

notes.title: March 11
notes.body:
The standup went longer than expected. We need a better format.
Separate thought: I've been thinking about switching to async updates entirely. Fewer meetings, more written communication. Worth proposing.

Also remembered I need to renew the car registration this month.

notes.title: March 12
notes.body:
Quiet day. Mostly heads-down work.

Read a bit in the evening — halfway through the book now. The author's argument about attention is interesting but feels overstated.

Need to be more consistent about writing here. Even five minutes helps.

Spent the morning going through backlog tickets. Most of them were stale and could just be closed.

Had a long call about the Q3 roadmap. Still not sure we're aligned on priorities, but at least the disagreements are visible now instead of buried.

Loose ends to close out this week:
  • Follow up on the pending PR reviews
  • Book the dentist appointment
  • Reply to the email thread about the offsite
  • Renew the domain before it expires
  • Look into the noise-cancelling headphones

Lunch with Priya — good to catch up outside of work context for once.

Tried a new running route along the river. Longer than usual, legs are feeling it now.

Started reading about spaced repetition for note-taking. Might be worth restructuring how I tag things here.

Small win: finally fixed that flaky test that's been failing intermittently for two weeks.

Dinner conversation turned into a long debate about whether remote work has actually made collaboration worse or just different. No conclusion, as usual.

Watched a documentary about deep sea creatures. The bioluminescence stuff is wild.

Woke up early without an alarm for once. Should try to protect that instead of immediately filling the extra time.

Draft of the proposal is done. Sitting on it overnight before sending — always catch more issues with fresh eyes.

Rough plan for tomorrow, in order:
  1. Look into the noise-cancelling headphones
  2. Renew the domain before it expires
  3. Reply to the email thread about the offsite
  4. Book the dentist appointment
  5. Follow up on the pending PR reviews

Argument with myself about whether to buy the expensive noise-cancelling headphones. Probably will end up getting them anyway.

Good session at the gym. Progressive overload is finally showing on the numbers, not just how it feels.

Spent too long doom-scrolling before bed. Need to actually put the phone in another room.

Team retro today — the format still feels a bit stiff, people hold back until the last ten minutes.

Cooked something new for dinner, turned out better than expected. Writing down the recipe so I don't lose it.

Long walk with no destination in mind. Some of my better thinking happens exactly like this, unplanned.

Reflecting on the last few weeks — busier than I'd like, but the busyness has mostly been meaningful, not just noise.

notes.title: Q2 Planning notes
notes.body:
Key themes for Q2:
  1. Ship the mobile improvements
  2. Reduce support backlog by 40%
  3. Hire one more engineer

Open questions:
  • Do we prioritise the API or the dashboard first?
  • What's the timeline for the data migration?
Risks: the contractor finishes in April, bandwidth is tight until then.

notes.title: App ideas
notes.body:
Random ideas, unfiltered:
  • Offline-first sync with CRDTs
  • End-to-end encrypted notes — keys stay on device
  • Keyboard shortcuts for everything
  • Weekly digest email: your notes from this week last year

The digest idea is the most interesting. Low effort, high value.
